seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using Vercel AI SDK face heightened risks due to the potential for AI hallucinations in case citations and breaches of attorney-client privilege. Without compliance guardrails, streaming responses can expose partial hallucinations or unvalidated tool calls, leading to malpractice suits or bar sanctions as seen in the Mata v. Avianca precedent. Vex provides essential middleware to ensure AI-generated legal content meets bar ethics rules, court filing standards, and eDiscovery protocols.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must never be exposed in AI responses or used for model training.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware scans streaming outputs for PII and privileged data, blocking responses containing sensitive client details before they reach the UI.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice must be reviewed by a licensed attorney and cannot be presented as final counsel.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces disclaimers in every response and flags unverified legal claims for attorney review.

complianceRequirements.description: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated citations are grounds for sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates case citations against legal databases in real-time, rejecting hallucinated references like non-existent precedents.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools must preserve chain-of-custody for document analysis and prevent data leakage.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all tool calls and responses with tamper-proof audit trails compliant with eDiscovery requirements.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: AI generates fake precedents leading to court sanctions or case dismissal.
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca: Lawyer sanctioned for citing non-existent cases from ChatGPT.

industryRisks.name: Privilege breach via streaming
industryRisks.description: Partial streaming responses leak client confidential information before full validation.
industryRisks.example: Streaming UI displays client SSN in first chunk before filters activate.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated tool call results
industryRisks.description: Legal research tools return unverified data displayed directly to users.
industryRisks.example: Westlaw API mock returns fabricated statute text passed to attorney.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent structured outputs
industryRisks.description: Different LLM providers format legal citations differently, causing parsing failures.
industryRisks.example: OpenAI formats Bluebook citations differently than Anthropic, breaking court filing parser.

implementation.title: Add Vex Middleware to generateText
implementation.description: Wrap Vercel AI SDK's generateText with Vex to validate legal citations and block privilege breaches before response completion.

implementation.title: Secure Streaming with streamText
implementation.description: Protect streaming UIs from partial hallucinations by validating each chunk for legal compliance.

implementation.title: Validate Tool Calls in Legal Agents
implementation.description: Ensure case law research tools return verified results before displaying to attorneys.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deploys Vercel AI SDK to power their case intake portal, using streaming UIs for real-time legal research and document analysis. Junior associates use the AI-powered interface during client consultations to quickly pull relevant precedents and draft initial pleadings. The system integrates with Westlaw and LexisNexis via tool calls, displaying streaming results directly in React Server Components for immediate attorney review.

Without compliance guardrails, the first week reveals critical failures: hallucinated citations appear in streaming responses (e.g., 'Smith v. Jones, 2025'), client names briefly flash in partial streams before filters catch them, and inconsistent structured outputs from different LLM providers break the firm's court filing parser. A partner nearly files a motion citing a fake precedent, risking sanctions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex middleware, all streaming responses are validated chunk-by-chunk for privilege breaches and citation accuracy. Tool call results from legal databases are verified against real case law before display, with automatic disclaimers added requiring attorney review. The firm processes 300% more cases without compliance incidents, passing a bar association AI audit with zero findings. Hallucinated citations dropped to 0%, and eDiscovery logs provide perfect chain-of-custody for all AI-assisted work products.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Vercel AI SDK deployment today to eliminate hallucination risks and ensure bar-compliant legal AI.
